options.content:
# W&B Async Jobs & Lifecycle

Use this when a customer reports **"old run data is slow or missing, and storage keeps growing."** It shows the background jobs that move finished run data into Parquet and the housekeeping crons (log deletion, run/file garbage collection, sweep early-stop).

**Start here, in order:** (1) **Parquet export lag** — is recent run data behind? (2) **Parquet throughput & duration** — are the export tasks actually running and finishing? (3) **Cron & background jobs** — are the housekeeping jobs backing up?

**NOTE — artifact garbage collection does NOT live here.** For artifact/blob GC and object-storage cleanup use the [W&B Artifacts](/d/wandb-artifacts) dashboard. For the stores' own CPU/memory/disk see [W&B Managed Install Performance](/d/wandb-managed-install-performance).

Use the **W&B Namespace** dropdown above to switch installs.

panels.title: Parquet export lag (history & events)
panels.description: TODO: DogStatsD metric — currently EMPTY because gorilla runs with GORILLA_STATSD_PORT=0, which disables DogStatsD emission (a core/manifest-side setting, not the operator); it populates once that port is set. Names may carry a service prefix; if still empty after enabling, check label_values(__name__). Gauges export_history_to_parquet_lag / export_events_to_parquet_lag.

options.content:
**What it shows** — how far behind the Parquet export jobs are: the gap between the newest run/event data and what has been written to Parquet for fast historical queries.

**What bad looks like** — lag climbing steadily and not coming back down (e.g. rising for more than ~30 min, or much higher than usual for this install).

**Reassure the customer** — high lag means **recent run data is slow to query, NOT lost.** It self-heals once throughput catches up.

**Do next** — check **Parquet throughput & duration** below: if tasks are completing but lag stays high, the store is the bottleneck (see [Managed Install Performance](/d/wandb-managed-install-performance)); if tasks aren't running at all, snapshot + escalate.

panels.title: Parquet task started vs completed (per sec)
panels.description: TODO: DogStatsD metric — currently EMPTY because gorilla runs with GORILLA_STATSD_PORT=0, which disables DogStatsD emission (a core/manifest-side setting, not the operator); it populates once that port is set. Names may carry a service prefix; if still empty after enabling, check label_values(__name__). Counters export_history_to_parquet_task_started/_task_completed and export_events_to_parquet_*.

options.content:
**What it shows** — the rate at which export tasks are **started** vs **completed**, for both run history and events.

**What bad looks like** — `completed` sitting well below `started` (tasks pile up and don't finish), or both flat at zero while lag (panel above) is high (the worker isn't running).

**Do next** — completed tracking started but lag still high → the store is slow, check the duration panel and [Managed Install Performance](/d/wandb-managed-install-performance). Both flat at zero → the export worker is down; snapshot this panel + the export pod logs and escalate.

panels.title: Parquet task & export duration (avg / p95)
panels.description: TODO: DogStatsD metric — currently EMPTY because gorilla runs with GORILLA_STATSD_PORT=0, which disables DogStatsD emission (a core/manifest-side setting, not the operator); it populates once that port is set. Names may carry a service prefix; if still empty after enabling, check label_values(__name__). Histogram export_history_to_parquet_task_duration (_bucket/_sum/_count) and export_history_to_parquet_export_duration.

options.content:
**What it shows** — how long individual export tasks take (avg and p95) and the overall export duration.

**What bad looks like** — p95 climbing well above its normal band for this install, or a sudden step-up after a version change (see **Service Image Versions** on [Field Investigation](/d/wandb-field-investigation)).

**Do next** — durations rising alongside store pressure → the DB/object-store is slow, check [Managed Install Performance](/d/wandb-managed-install-performance) and the [Data Stores app view](/d/wandb-datastores). If durations are normal but lag is high, the worker just isn't keeping up with volume; snapshot + escalate.

panels.title: Parquet compaction & deletion (per sec)
panels.description: TODO: DogStatsD metric — currently EMPTY because gorilla runs with GORILLA_STATSD_PORT=0, which disables DogStatsD emission (a core/manifest-side setting, not the operator); it populates once that port is set. Names may carry a service prefix; if still empty after enabling, check label_values(__name__). Counters export_*_to_parquet_compact_completed / _delete_completed.

options.content:
**What it shows** — the maintenance side of Parquet export: compaction (merging small files) and deletion of superseded intermediate files.

**What bad looks like** — compaction/deletion flat at zero while export volume is high — small files accumulate and **storage grows** (the customer's "storage keeps growing" symptom).

**Do next** — if these are stalled, snapshot this panel and check the export worker/pod logs, then escalate. For object-store growth from artifacts specifically, use the [W&B Artifacts](/d/wandb-artifacts) dashboard.

panels.title: Cron seconds behind
panels.description: TODO: DogStatsD metric — currently EMPTY because gorilla runs with GORILLA_STATSD_PORT=0, which disables DogStatsD emission (a core/manifest-side setting, not the operator); it populates once that port is set. Names may carry a service prefix; if still empty after enabling, check label_values(__name__). Gauge cron_seconds_behind (per-job label name may differ).

options.content:
**What it shows** — how far behind schedule the background cron jobs are running.

**What bad looks like** — `cron_seconds_behind` **climbing and not recovering** = jobs are backing up. This is the clearest signal that housekeeping (log/run cleanup, sweeps) has stalled.

**Do next** — if it keeps climbing, **snapshot this panel and escalate** — a stuck cron scheduler affects deletion and cleanup across the install. Check which job label is worst and correlate with the deletion/GC panels below.

panels.title: Garbage collection & log deletion (per sec)
panels.description: TODO: DogStatsD metric — currently EMPTY because gorilla runs with GORILLA_STATSD_PORT=0, which disables DogStatsD emission (a core/manifest-side setting, not the operator); it populates once that port is set. Names may carry a service prefix; if still empty after enabling, check label_values(__name__). Counters garbage_collect_files/_runs/_runs_v2, delete_logs, delete_logs_error.

options.content:
**What it shows** — the housekeeping jobs that reclaim space: file/run garbage collection and log deletion, plus the log-deletion error counter.

**What bad looks like** — `delete logs error` above zero, or GC rates dropping to zero while storage grows. Errors mean cleanup is failing and disk will keep filling.

**Do next** — persistent `delete_logs_error` → snapshot + escalate. Note: this is run/file/log cleanup, **not** artifact blob GC — for that use the [W&B Artifacts](/d/wandb-artifacts) dashboard.

panels.title: Sweep early-stop activity (per sec)
panels.description: TODO: DogStatsD metric — currently EMPTY because gorilla runs with GORILLA_STATSD_PORT=0, which disables DogStatsD emission (a core/manifest-side setting, not the operator); it populates once that port is set. Names may carry a service prefix; if still empty after enabling, check label_values(__name__). Counters early_stop_sweeps_stopped/_skipped/_error.

options.content:
**What it shows** — the sweep early-stopping cron: runs it stopped, runs it skipped, and errors.

**What bad looks like** — a rising `error` line, or `stopped`/`skipped` flat at zero when the customer expects sweeps to early-stop (the job may not be running).

**Do next** — if `error` is non-zero or the customer reports sweeps not stopping, snapshot this panel and the sweep/cron pod logs and escalate. Correlate onset with **Cron seconds behind** above.

panels.title: Top parquet/glue spans by call count (sampled)

options.content:
**What it shows** — trace-derived (spanmetrics) call counts for spans whose name mentions parquet or glue. This is the export/compaction work as seen from tracing.

**Note** — spanmetrics are **not** namespace-scoped and are **sampled**: trust the *shape* (which span dominates), not exact counts.

**Do next** — to see an actual slow/failed export, use **Open Traces in Explore** (top-right) and filter by the span name shown here. If this panel is empty, the span name pattern may differ on your stack — check `label_values(traces_spanmetrics_calls_milliseconds_total, span_name)`.
